sections.paragraph:
ῥίς, ῥινός, ἡ (Hom. et al.; pap fr. III b.c.; LXX [only pl. ῥῖνες]; ApcEsdr 6:7f [pl.]; Philo [pl.]) nose; pl. nostrils, i.e. nose (so Hom. et al.) ἐὰν μὴ τὰς ῥ. ταῖς χερσὶν ἐπιφράξῃ without covering one’s nose with one’s hands Pa (3:3).—DELG.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

  1. fr. = from

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

  1. pl. = plural

ApcEsdr ApcEsdr = Apocalypse of Esdras—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

i.e.

i.e. = id est (that is)

I.-E. Indo-European

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 906
